report_metadata.cancer_type: Non-Small Cell Lung Cancer — Squamous Cell Carcinoma

treatments.name: Surgical Resection (Lobectomy) + Adjuvant Cisplatin-Vinorelbine Chemotherapy

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 RCTs (JBR-10, ANITA) and LACE meta-analysis of 4,584 patients demonstrate clear survival benefit for adjuvant cisplatin-vinorelbine in completely resected stage II NSCLC

survival_benefit.rationale: LACE meta-analysis showed 11.6% absolute 5-year survival improvement for stage II NSCLC with cisplatin-vinorelbine (HR 0.80, 95% CI 0.70-0.91, p<0.001). 5-year OS for stage II is approximately 60-65% with surgery plus adjuvant chemo

accessibility.rationale: FDA-approved, NCCN Category 1 recommendation, universally available at all cancer treatment centers worldwide

safety_profile.rationale: Well-characterized toxicity profile. Grade 3/4 neutropenia is common (~73%). Cisplatin causes nephrotoxicity, ototoxicity, nausea. Heavy alcohol use may increase hepatotoxicity risk requiring close liver function monitoring

biomarker_match.rationale: No biomarker requirement — universally applicable to all resectable stage II NSCLC regardless of PD-L1 or FGFR1 status

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Lobectomy removes the primary tumor with adequate margins. Cisplatin cross-links DNA causing apoptosis; vinorelbine inhibits microtubule assembly blocking mitosis. Together they eliminate micrometastatic disease post-resection.
notable_side_effects: Hepatotoxicity risk elevated with heavy alcohol use

treatments.name: Perioperative Nivolumab + Platinum-Based Chemotherapy (CheckMate 816/77T Regimen)

evidence_level.rationale: Two Phase 3 RCTs support this approach: CheckMate 816 (neoadjuvant, N=358) and CheckMate 77T (perioperative, N=461). Both demonstrated statistically significant EFS/pCR improvement. FDA-approved based on Phase 3 data

survival_benefit.rationale: CheckMate 816: 4-year OS 71% vs 58% (13% absolute improvement); CheckMate 77T: median EFS 40.1 vs 17.0 months (HR 0.59, 95% CI 0.45-0.79). pCR rates substantially improved. Benefit consistent across squamous histology subgroups

accessibility.rationale: FDA-approved for neoadjuvant nivolumab + chemotherapy in resectable stage IIA-IIIB NSCLC. Perioperative nivolumab also FDA-approved. Available at major cancer centers. Stage II is within labeled indication

safety_profile.rationale: Addition of nivolumab did not increase incidence of adverse events or impede surgery feasibility. Immune-related AEs include pneumonitis, hepatitis, colitis. Heavy alcohol use requires hepatic monitoring during immunotherapy

biomarker_match.rationale: Benefit observed across PD-L1 subgroups including TPS 1-49%. No EGFR/ALK mutations required for eligibility. Patient's PD-L1 TPS 1-49% is within the benefiting population

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Nivolumab is a PD-1 immune checkpoint inhibitor that blocks PD-1/PD-L1 interaction, reactivating T-cell antitumor immunity. Combined with platinum-based chemotherapy in the neoadjuvant setting, it generates pathologic complete responses and eliminates micrometastatic disease. Perioperative approach adds adjuvant nivolumab monotherapy post-surgery.

treatments.name: Neoadjuvant Nivolumab + Chemotherapy (CheckMate 816 Regimen)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 CheckMate 816 (N=358) published in NEJM. Landmark trial establishing neoadjuvant chemoimmunotherapy as standard. 4-year follow-up confirms sustained OS benefit

survival_benefit.rationale: 4-year OS 71% vs 58% (13% absolute improvement). Significantly higher pCR rate. EFS significantly improved. Benefit consistent across squamous histology subgroup

accessibility.rationale: FDA-approved for neoadjuvant treatment of resectable stage IB-IIIA NSCLC. Widely available, though requires coordination of neoadjuvant immunotherapy with surgical planning

safety_profile.rationale: No increase in adverse events vs chemotherapy alone. Did not impede feasibility of surgery. Well-tolerated neoadjuvant regimen. Alcohol-related hepatic monitoring still recommended

biomarker_match.rationale: Benefit across PD-L1 subgroups. Squamous histology well-represented (51% of patients). No EGFR/ALK exclusion needed for squamous. PD-L1 TPS 1-49% included in benefiting population

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Neoadjuvant nivolumab (anti-PD-1) combined with platinum-based chemotherapy for 3 cycles prior to surgical resection. Immunotherapy primes systemic antitumor immunity while chemotherapy induces immunogenic cell death, potentially generating superior pathologic responses and eliminating micrometastases.
notable_side_effects: Grade 3/4 treatment-related AEs comparable to chemo alone

treatments.name: Perioperative Durvalumab + Platinum-Based Chemotherapy (AEGEAN Regimen)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 AEGEAN trial (N=802) demonstrated statistically significant improvement in EFS and pCR with perioperative durvalumab in resectable stage IIA-IIIB NSCLC. Published in NEJM 2023. FDA-approved

survival_benefit.rationale: 32% reduction in risk of disease progression/recurrence/death (HR 0.68). pCR rate 17.0% vs 4.3% with placebo. Benefit observed in both squamous and nonsquamous histology subgroups

accessibility.rationale: FDA-approved for neoadjuvant/adjuvant use in resectable NSCLC stage IIA-IIIB. Widely available at oncology centers

safety_profile.rationale: Grade 3/4 AEs similar between durvalumab (42.3%) and placebo (43.4%) arms, indicating no additional safety burden from immunotherapy. Immune-related hepatitis requires monitoring given patient's heavy alcohol use

biomarker_match.rationale: Benefit seen across PD-L1 subgroups. Patient's PD-L1 TPS 1-49% falls within benefiting population. No EGFR/ALK mutation exclusion relevant for squamous histology

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Durvalumab is a PD-L1 immune checkpoint inhibitor that blocks PD-L1 on tumor cells from engaging PD-1 on T cells, restoring antitumor immune response. Given neoadjuvantly with chemotherapy for 4 cycles, followed by surgery, then adjuvant durvalumab monotherapy for up to 12 cycles.

treatments.name: Adjuvant Pembrolizumab Post-Surgery and Chemotherapy (KEYNOTE-091 Regimen)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 KEYNOTE-091/PEARLS trial (N=1177) demonstrated significant DFS improvement in ITT population. FDA-approved for adjuvant treatment of stage IB-IIIA NSCLC after complete resection and platinum-based chemotherapy

survival_benefit.rationale: Median DFS 53.8 vs 43.0 months (HR 0.81, 95% CI 0.68-0.96). 4-year DFS 52.4% vs 45.7%. Subgroup analysis showed benefit primarily in stage II patients with PD-L1 1-49% — matching this patient's profile. OS data not yet mature

accessibility.rationale: FDA-approved, widely available. Pembrolizumab is the most commonly used checkpoint inhibitor globally with extensive clinical infrastructure

safety_profile.rationale: Well-established safety profile for pembrolizumab. Immune-mediated adverse events manageable with guidelines. Heavy alcohol use requires liver function monitoring during treatment

biomarker_match.rationale: Approved regardless of PD-L1 status, but subgroup analysis shows strongest DFS benefit in PD-L1 TPS 1-49% cohort — precisely matching this patient. Stage II subgroup also showed preferential benefit

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Pembrolizumab is a humanized monoclonal antibody targeting PD-1 on T cells, blocking interaction with PD-L1/PD-L2 ligands, thereby reactivating adaptive immune responses against residual tumor cells after surgical resection and adjuvant chemotherapy.
biomarker_requirements: No specific biomarker required (FDA-approved for all PD-L1 levels)

treatments.name: Adjuvant Atezolizumab Post-Surgery and Chemotherapy (IMpower010 Regimen)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 IMpower010 trial (N=1005) with 5-year follow-up data published 2025. First adjuvant immunotherapy trial to report >=5-year survival data. Demonstrated DFS and OS benefit in PD-L1 TC >=1% population

survival_benefit.rationale: In stage II-IIIA PD-L1 TC >=1%: DFS HR 0.70, OS HR 0.77. PD-L1 TC >=50%: DFS HR 0.48, OS HR 0.47. For PD-L1 1-49% range (this patient), benefit is more modest with HR ~0.83. 5-year DFS and OS data now mature

accessibility.rationale: FDA-approved for adjuvant treatment of stage II-IIIA NSCLC with PD-L1 TC >=1% after complete resection and platinum-based chemotherapy

safety_profile.rationale: No new safety signals at 5-year follow-up. Manageable immune-related AEs. Hepatic monitoring important given patient's heavy alcohol use

biomarker_match.rationale: Requires PD-L1 TC >=1% for FDA label. Patient's PD-L1 TPS 1-49% qualifies but greatest benefit seen at PD-L1 >=50%. Benefit in 1-49% range is more limited compared to pembrolizumab KEYNOTE-091 in this subgroup

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Atezolizumab is an anti-PD-L1 monoclonal antibody that blocks PD-L1 on tumor cells and tumor-infiltrating immune cells from binding PD-1 and B7.1 receptors, thereby restoring T-cell mediated antitumor immunity. Administered as adjuvant monotherapy after surgical resection and adjuvant chemotherapy.

treatments.name: Cemiplimab Monotherapy (for PD-L1 >=50% or with Chemotherapy)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 EMPOWER-Lung 1 and EMPOWER-Lung 3 trials demonstrate efficacy in advanced NSCLC. 5-year follow-up data available. However, no perioperative/adjuvant trial data yet for early-stage NSCLC

survival_benefit.rationale: In advanced NSCLC with PD-L1 >=50%: median OS not reached vs 14.2 months (HR 0.57). With chemotherapy in PD-L1 >=1%: improved OS. However, no stage II-specific data and patient's PD-L1 is 1-49%, limiting monotherapy benefit

accessibility.rationale: FDA-approved for advanced NSCLC (PD-L1 >=50% monotherapy, all-comers with chemotherapy). Not yet approved for perioperative/adjuvant early-stage NSCLC. Off-label for this stage II indication

safety_profile.rationale: Similar safety profile to other PD-1/PD-L1 inhibitors. Immune-mediated AEs manageable. Alcohol use requires hepatic monitoring

biomarker_match.rationale: Monotherapy approved for PD-L1 >=50% only; patient's TPS is 1-49%. Combination with chemotherapy has broader PD-L1 coverage but only in advanced setting

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Cemiplimab is a fully human PD-1 blocking antibody that restores T-cell antitumor activity by preventing PD-1/PD-L1 interaction. Approved in advanced NSCLC as monotherapy (PD-L1 >=50%) or with chemotherapy. Potential perioperative use being explored.
biomarker_requirements: Any PD-L1 for combination with chemotherapy
treatments.availability: FDA Approved (advanced NSCLC); Off-label for early-stage adjuvant use

treatments.name: Necitumumab + Gemcitabine-Cisplatin (SQUIRE Regimen)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 3 SQUIRE trial (N=1093) demonstrated statistically significant OS improvement. FDA-approved for first-line metastatic squamous NSCLC. However, approved only for advanced/metastatic disease, not early-stage

survival_benefit.rationale: Modest OS improvement: 11.5 vs 9.9 months (HR 0.84, p=0.012) in metastatic disease. No data in early-stage/adjuvant setting. 1.6-month median OS gain is clinically modest

accessibility.rationale: FDA-approved for metastatic squamous NSCLC but limited current clinical use due to evolving treatment landscape. Not indicated for stage II disease

safety_profile.rationale: Increased thromboembolic events, hypomagnesemia, skin rash. Addition of anti-EGFR antibody to chemotherapy adds toxicity. Hypertension comorbidity is relevant concern

biomarker_match.rationale: Targets EGFR pathway which is commonly expressed in squamous NSCLC. No specific biomarker required. However, EGFR amplification/overexpression correlation with benefit is uncertain

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Necitumumab is a recombinant human IgG1 monoclonal antibody that binds EGFR, blocking ligand binding and receptor activation. Inhibits EGFR-driven proliferation and survival signaling in squamous NSCLC cells. Combined with gemcitabine-cisplatin chemotherapy.

treatments.name: Erdafitinib (FGFR Inhibitor — Investigational for FGFR1-Amplified Squamous NSCLC)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 FIND trial and NCI-MATCH EAY131-K1 subprotocol evaluated erdafitinib in FGFR-altered squamous NSCLC. NCI-MATCH K1 showed no confirmed responses in FGFR-amplified tumors (0/18), though stable disease observed in 5 patients

survival_benefit.rationale: No confirmed responses to erdafitinib in FGFR1-amplified population per NCI-MATCH K1. Limited antitumor activity in FGFR amplification vs. FGFR mutations/fusions. Not a proven survival benefit in this setting

accessibility.rationale: FDA-approved for FGFR-altered urothelial carcinoma only. Not approved for lung cancer. Available through clinical trials or compassionate use only

safety_profile.rationale: Known FGFR class effects: hyperphosphatemia, stomatitis, nail changes, dry eyes, diarrhea. Generally manageable but adds hepatotoxic burden — relevant given patient's heavy alcohol use

biomarker_match.rationale: Patient has FGFR1 amplification, a potential target. However, FGFR1 amplification has shown weaker predictive value for FGFR inhibitor response compared to FGFR2/3 mutations or fusions

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Erdafitinib is a selective pan-FGFR (1-4) tyrosine kinase inhibitor that blocks FGFR signaling, inhibiting tumor cell proliferation and survival in FGFR-driven cancers. Targets the amplified FGFR1 pathway in squamous NSCLC.
biomarker_requirements: FGFR gene alteration (amplification, mutation, or fusion)
treatments.availability: Clinical Trial / Investigational for Lung Cancer

treatments.name: Infigratinib (FGFR Inhibitor — Investigational for FGFR1-Amplified Squamous NSCLC)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 1 data in 36 FGFR1-amplified squamous NSCLC patients showed 4 partial responses and 14 stable disease cases. Limited sample size. No Phase 3 data

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR ~11% (4/36) in FGFR1-amplified squamous NSCLC with additional 39% stable disease rate, suggesting some clinical activity but modest antitumor response

accessibility.rationale: Not widely available. Development has been discontinuous. Clinical trial access only. FDA previously accelerated approval for cholangiocarcinoma but this was later withdrawn

safety_profile.rationale: Typical FGFR inhibitor class toxicity. Hyperphosphatemia, stomatitis manageable. Hepatic monitoring recommended for patient with heavy alcohol use

biomarker_match.rationale: Specifically tested in FGFR1-amplified squamous NSCLC population. Showed some activity unlike erdafitinib in NCI-MATCH, suggesting potential differential sensitivity

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Infigratinib (BGJ398) is a selective oral pan-FGFR inhibitor that competitively blocks FGFR1-3 kinase activity, inhibiting downstream RAS-MAPK and PI3K-AKT signaling in FGFR1-amplified tumor cells.

treatments.name: Futibatinib (FGFR Inhibitor — Investigational for FGFR1-Amplified Squamous NSCLC)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 1/2 data in broader FGFR-altered solid tumors (ORR 41.7%). No dedicated Phase 3 trial in FGFR1-amplified squamous NSCLC. FDA-approved only for FGFR2 fusion cholangiocarcinoma

survival_benefit.rationale: ORR 41.7% in FGFR-altered solid tumors, but this primarily reflects FGFR2 fusions. FGFR1 amplification responses are less robust. No NSCLC-specific survival data available

accessibility.rationale: Not approved for lung cancer. Available through clinical trials only. FDA-approved for cholangiocarcinoma with FGFR2 fusion

safety_profile.rationale: Irreversible FGFR inhibitor with known class effects. Hyperphosphatemia, nail changes, dry eyes, stomatitis. Hepatotoxicity monitoring needed especially with heavy alcohol use

biomarker_match.rationale: Targets FGFR1 amplification but covalent binding mechanism may offer advantage over reversible inhibitors. However, clinical evidence for FGFR1 amplification response is limited

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Futibatinib is an irreversible (covalent) pan-FGFR inhibitor that binds the FGFR kinase domain irreversibly, potentially overcoming resistance to reversible FGFR inhibitors. May provide more sustained FGFR pathway inhibition in FGFR1-amplified tumors.
treatments.availability: Clinical Trial / Investigational for Lung Cancer

treatments.name: Pemigatinib (FGFR Inhibitor — Investigational for FGFR-Aberrant NSCLC)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 1b case series in FGFR-aberrant advanced NSCLC. Only 2 cases reported with prolonged clinical benefit. No controlled trial data in lung cancer

survival_benefit.rationale: Anecdotal prolonged clinical benefit in 2 cases of FGFR-aberrant advanced NSCLC as second-line therapy. Insufficient data to quantify survival benefit

accessibility.rationale: FDA-approved for FGFR2 fusion cholangiocarcinoma and myeloid/lymphoid neoplasms. Not approved for lung cancer. Clinical trial access only

safety_profile.rationale: Well-tolerated in reported cases. Standard FGFR class effects. Hepatotoxicity monitoring needed given patient's alcohol use

biomarker_match.rationale: Targets FGFR aberrations including amplification. Patient's FGFR1 amplification is a potential target but limited lung cancer-specific data

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Pemigatinib is a selective oral inhibitor of FGFR1, FGFR2, and FGFR3. It blocks FGFR phosphorylation and downstream signaling, inhibiting proliferation in FGFR-driven tumors. Shows selectivity that may spare FGFR4, potentially reducing certain toxicities.

key_evidence.study_name: Phase Ib FGFR-aberrant NSCLC Case Series

biomarker_requirements: FGFR gene alteration (amplification, mutation, or fusion)
treatments.availability: Clinical Trial / Investigational for Lung Cancer

treatments.name: Rogaratinib (Pan-FGFR Inhibitor — FGFR mRNA Overexpression)

evidence_level.rationale: Phase 2 SAKK 19/18 trial in advanced pretreated squamous NSCLC overexpressing FGFR mRNA. Trial closed early for futility — only 1/15 patients achieved 6-month PFS

survival_benefit.rationale: Median PFS only 1.6 months and median OS 3.5 months. Trial terminated early due to lack of efficacy. Development in squamous lung cancer put on hold by Bayer

accessibility.rationale: Development halted for squamous NSCLC indication. Not available through clinical trials for this cancer type. Research compound only

safety_profile.rationale: Standard FGFR inhibitor toxicity profile. Manageable AEs but hepatotoxicity monitoring needed

biomarker_match.rationale: Selected patients based on FGFR mRNA overexpression rather than FGFR1 amplification. Different biomarker selection strategy may explain poor results vs. genomic amplification

treatments.mechanism_of_action: Rogaratinib is a potent and selective oral pan-FGFR inhibitor. Uniquely selected patients by FGFR mRNA overexpression rather than genomic alteration. This biomarker-selection approach was tested in squamous NSCLC but did not demonstrate clinical benefit.

clinical_trials.title: CheckMate 77T: Perioperative Nivolumab + Chemotherapy in Resectable NSCLC
clinical_trials.early_results: Median EFS 40.1 vs 17.0 months (HR 0.59). pCR significantly improved. Squamous subgroup showed consistent benefit. FDA approved perioperative nivolumab based on this trial.

clinical_trials.title: AEGEAN: Perioperative Durvalumab + Chemotherapy in Resectable Stage II-IIIB NSCLC
clinical_trials.early_results: EFS HR 0.68 (32% risk reduction). pCR 17.0% vs 4.3%. Similar benefit in squamous and nonsquamous histology. FDA approved.

clinical_trials.title: FIND: Erdafitinib in FGFR-Altered Squamous NSCLC
clinical_trials.early_results: Screened patients with FGFR alterations in squamous NSCLC. Three cohorts: high-confidence translocations, high-confidence mutations, low-confidence alterations. Modest clinical activity observed.

clinical_trials.title: NCI-MATCH EAY131 Subprotocol K1: Erdafitinib in FGFR-Amplified Tumors
clinical_trials.early_results: 35 patients enrolled, 18 evaluable. No confirmed responses. 5 patients with stable disease. Trial met its negative endpoint for FGFR-amplified tumors.

clinical_trials.title: Infigratinib (BGJ398) Phase I Expansion in FGFR1-Amplified Squamous NSCLC
clinical_trials.early_results: 36 FGFR1-amplified squamous NSCLC patients: 4 partial responses (ORR 11%), 14 stable disease (DCR 50%). Showed modest but detectable antitumor activity.

clinical_trials.title: SAKK 19/18: Rogaratinib in FGFR mRNA-Overexpressing Squamous NSCLC
clinical_trials.early_results: 15 patients treated. Only 1 patient achieved 6-month PFS. Median PFS 1.6 months, median OS 3.5 months. Closed early for futility.

clinical_trials.title: Neoadjuvant Dupilumab and Cemiplimab in Early-Stage Resectable NSCLC
clinical_trials.early_results: Novel combination of IL-4/IL-13 blockade (dupilumab) with anti-PD-1 (cemiplimab) in neoadjuvant setting. Preliminary results pending.

combination_strategies.rationale: Chemotherapy induces immunogenic cell death releasing tumor antigens, while nivolumab releases immune checkpoint inhibition. Synergistic effect produces pathologic complete responses (24% vs 2.2%) and improved EFS (HR 0.59). Standard of care combination for neoadjuvant treatment of resectable NSCLC.

Perioperative approach combines neoadjuvant chemoimmunotherapy to shrink tumor and prime immunity, with adjuvant checkpoint inhibitor maintenance to prevent recurrence. pCR 17% vs 4.3%, EFS HR 0.68. Addresses both local and systemic disease.

Platinum-based chemotherapy eliminates rapidly dividing residual cancer cells, then checkpoint inhibitor maintains immune surveillance against micrometastatic disease. Supported by KEYNOTE-091 showing strongest DFS benefit in stage II with PD-L1 1-49% when given after adjuvant chemotherapy.

FGFR pathway activation may contribute to immune evasion and immunotherapy resistance. Combining FGFR inhibitor with checkpoint inhibitor may overcome this resistance mechanism. Preclinical data suggest FGFR inhibition can enhance T-cell infiltration. Multiple early-phase trials exploring this combination in FGFR-altered tumors.

IL-4/IL-13 blockade with dupilumab may reprogram the tumor microenvironment by reducing type 2 inflammation and immunosuppressive M2 macrophages. Combined with PD-1 blockade, this dual immune modulation may enhance antitumor immunity. Novel approach being tested at Mount Sinai.

supportive_care.approach: Structured Exercise and Pulmonary Rehabilitation Program
supportive_care.evidence: Comprehensive meta-analysis (GeroScience, 2025) of cancer patients showed 24% lower risk of cancer-specific death with physical activity. In NSCLC specifically, each 50-meter improvement in 6-minute walk test associated with 13% reduction in death risk. Exercise significantly reduces cancer-related fatigue and improves quality of life.
supportive_care.benefit: 24% reduction in cancer-specific mortality. Improved cardiorespiratory fitness, muscle strength, reduced fatigue and dyspnea. Perioperative exercise prehabilitation reduces postoperative pulmonary complications. ECOG 0 patient is ideal candidate for intensive exercise program.

supportive_care.evidence: Systematic review and meta-analysis (PMC, 2025) found alcohol consumption during radiotherapy with or without chemotherapy associated with worse disease-free survival. Alcohol increases hepatotoxicity risk during cisplatin-based chemotherapy, increases surgical complication rates, and may worsen immunotherapy-related hepatitis. Heavy alcohol use elevates perioperative risk.
supportive_care.benefit: Reduced hepatotoxicity risk during chemotherapy, decreased surgical complications, improved disease-free survival, better tolerance of immunotherapy. Essential for safe cisplatin administration and checkpoint inhibitor use given hepatic clearance pathways.

supportive_care.evidence: Systematic review demonstrated current smokers undergoing lung cancer surgery have 86% increased risk for cancer recurrence and 2-fold decrease in 5-year survival vs quitters. Postoperative pulmonary complications 22% in smokers vs 2% in never-smokers. Minimum 6 weeks cessation before surgery recommended. Cost-effective intervention at 1 and 5 years.
supportive_care.benefit: 50% reduction in surgical complications, improved 5-year survival, reduced cancer recurrence risk, better wound healing. Even short preoperative cessation (4-8 weeks) improves pulmonary function and wound healing.

supportive_care.evidence: NCCN guidelines recommend careful preoperative cardiovascular assessment for lung resection candidates. Uncontrolled hypertension increases perioperative risk. Certain immunotherapy agents may cause hypertension. Cisplatin can cause nephrotoxicity which worsens hypertension. Anti-EGFR agents (necitumumab) carry thromboembolic risk.
supportive_care.benefit: Optimized blood pressure control reduces perioperative cardiovascular events, ensures safe administration of cisplatin (renal function preservation), and enables safe immunotherapy delivery. 45-year-old patient with ECOG 0 has good baseline for optimization.

supportive_care.evidence: Nutrition is identified as a core component of cancer rehabilitation programming (Frontiers in Cardiovascular Medicine meta-analysis). For patients with heavy alcohol use, hepatoprotective nutritional strategies including adequate protein intake, B-vitamin supplementation, and avoiding hepatotoxic supplements are recommended to minimize cisplatin and immunotherapy hepatotoxicity.
supportive_care.benefit: Improved treatment tolerance, reduced chemotherapy-related malnutrition, better surgical recovery, hepatic function preservation during treatment. Maintains performance status during multi-modal therapy.

supportive_care.evidence: Landmark NEJM study (Temel et al.) and subsequent evidence support concurrent palliative care from diagnosis in NSCLC. NCCN guidelines recommend offering palliative care concurrently with standard oncologic care. Studies show better quality of life and longer median survival with early palliative care integration.
supportive_care.benefit: Improved quality of life, better symptom management, potential survival benefit. Particularly relevant for managing treatment toxicities across surgery, chemotherapy, and immunotherapy. Supports psychological coping and treatment adherence.
